issuesFound.fix: Line 53: <button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="solutions-submenu" aria-haspopup="true">

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule sets outline: none but only provides outline-offset as a property. Since outline-offset applies to a zero-width outline, no visible focus indicator is rendered for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 318: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly removes the focus indicator for browsers or interactions that do not trigger :focus-visible, without providing any visual alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 325: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on @media (forced-colors: active) :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: In high-contrast mode, the focus indicator is explicitly set to outline: none. This prevents users with low vision from identifying the focused element in a mode specifically designed for visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 331: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component explicitly removes the outline on focus-visible without providing a replacement (like a box-shadow or border change) within the same rule block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 544: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, making it impossible for keyboard users to track focus on the brand link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 645: `.header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus indicator without replacement, blocking keyboard access to the menu on smaller screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 678: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the default outline. While it adds a border-color and box-shadow, the box-shadow uses a semi-transparent rgba value which may not be visible enough to serve as a valid focus replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 764: `#search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This rule explicitly sets outline: none for the search input on focus-visible with no replacement properties in the block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 771: `#search-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the focus indicator without replacement, hindering keyboard navigation within the search form.
issuesFound.fix: Line 793: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus indicator without providing a visible alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 816: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the focus indicator without replacement, making it difficult to navigate the features grid via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Product filter buttons remove the focus indicator without replacement, failing to show which category filter is currently focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1214: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing billing toggle buttons remove the focus indicator without replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1404: `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table region is focusable (tabindex="0") but removes its focus indicator, so keyboard users cannot see when they have focused the region to scroll it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1518: `.table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion triggers remove the focus indicator without replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1655: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form controls remove the default outline and replace it with a semi-transparent box-shadow which may lack sufficient contrast to be perceived as a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1711: `.form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form controls explicitly remove the focus indicator on focus-visible without replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1712: `.form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 absolute minimum of 24px for interactive targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 611: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons are explicitly sized to 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch and pointer targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 625: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class has a min-height of 32px. While it meets the 24px minimum, it falls below the 44px recommended target size without guaranteed spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 524: `.btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); ... }`

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input height is set to 28px, which is significantly below the 44px recommended size for primary interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 745: `#search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is sized at 28px by 28px, which is below the 44px recommendation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 780: `width: 44px; height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses width: auto and height: auto, and lacks the necessary clip/clip-path properties to hide content visually while keeping it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 351: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition for movement, but there is no override in the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes a transform transition for spatial movement (translateY on active), but this is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses a complex perspective transform and transition that triggers on hover, which can cause vestibular distress. The reduced motion block on line 945 is empty.
issuesFound.fix: Line 946: `.hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; }`

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body text is set to word-spacing: -0.2em, which crowds words together and significantly impairs readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 218: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability: body (letter-spacing)
issuesFound.explanation: The body text uses a negative letter-spacing of -0.05em, which reduces the legibility of character forms.
issuesFound.fix: Line 219: `letter-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1
issuesFound.explanation: The secondary text colour (--colour-text-secondary: #aaaaaa) used against the page background (--colour-bg-page: #ffffff) results in a contrast ratio of 2.3:1, which is well below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 85: `--colour-text-secondary: #707070;`

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.4:1
issuesFound.explanation: The error colour (--colour-error: #ee9999) used for the required field marker against a white background fails the 4.5:1 contrast ratio requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 76: `--colour-error: #c81e1e;`

issuesFound.title: link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule for <a> elements removes underlines. In body text (like .faq-item dd p a), links are only distinguishable by colour, which fails WCAG SC 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 245: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class on the drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1561: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function in the product filter module updates the `is-active` class on filter buttons but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute. Screen reader users cannot perceive which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2015: `const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds an error message and a CSS class but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid when it receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2915: `if (result.valid) { srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); ... } else { srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the hamburger trigger. ARIA states should be explicitly defined on load if the component is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader` module hides the header on scroll-down by adding a CSS class but does not apply `aria-hidden="true"`. Keyboard users can still accidentally tab into links within the hidden header.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: `_hidden = true; addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user toggles between 'grid' and 'list' view modes, the change is visual only. There is no live region announcement to inform screen reader users that the layout of the product list has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2065: `liveRegion.announce(mode === 'list' ? 'Switched to list view' : 'Switched to grid view');`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds a click listener to a `div` (the zoneEl) and assigns `role="button"`, but it does not provide a `keydown` handler for the `Space` key, only for `Enter`. Native buttons respond to both.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3255: `if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form email input (`#newsletter-email`) is a personal data field but lacks the `autocomplete` attribute required by WCAG 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2715: `const input = createElement('input', { type: 'email', id: 'newsletter-email', autocomplete: 'email' });`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation overlay (`_overlay`) is an interactive element that can be clicked to close the menu, but it has no accessible name or role, making it a "ghost" element for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1635: `_overlay = createElement('div', { class: 'nav-overlay', role: 'button', 'aria-label': 'Close menu' });`

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
Missing accessible name on interactive element

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle CSS classes and visibility but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `_open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1561: `_open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function in the `productFilter` module updates the visual state of filter tabs but fails to update the `aria-pressed` attribute. This prevents screen reader users from identifying the active product category filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds an error class and shows an error message but does not set the `aria-invalid="true"` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3195: `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is corrected and becomes valid, the `_renderResult` function removes the error class but does not reset the `aria-invalid` attribute to `false`. The field will continue to be announced as invalid by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3191: `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger. ARIA states should be explicitly defined on load so they are available before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader` module hides the header on scroll-down by adding a CSS class but does not apply `aria-hidden="true"`. Keyboard users navigating via "Alt+N" or similar shortcuts may still land on elements within a visually hidden header.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1865: `addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function in the `productFilter` module switches the grid between "list" and "grid" modes but does not announce this change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: `try { storage.set(...); } catch (_) {} liveRegion.announce("Switched to " + mode + " view");`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing` module allows `_toggleEl` to be a non-button element (like a `div` or `span`) and assigns it `role="switch"`, but it only handles `keydown` for Space and Enter. It fails to provide the required keyboard support for a switch role (which often includes arrow keys) and relies on manual tabindex.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires adding a comprehensive keydown handler for the switch role in the pricing module)

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on interactive element
issuesFound.explanation:
The `toastManager` creates a dismiss button with the text content "×". This character is often announced as "times" or "multiplication sign" rather than "Close" or "Dismiss", providing no clear purpose to screen reader users.
  Line 1515: `const dismissBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', className: classes.dismissBtn, [ARIA.LABEL]: STRINGS.labels.dismissToast || 'Dismiss' }, '×');`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The `contactForm` contains a "full_name" input field but does not include the `autocomplete="name"` attribute. This fails to assist users in filling out forms with personal data.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires adding autocomplete="name" to the input element in the HTML or via Line 3565: `if (field) { inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules); if(cfg.label === 'Full name') field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name'); }`)

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.init` function ensures `aria-expanded` is set but does not verify that the trigger button has an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the panel ID if it was missing in the source HTML.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2755: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, item.panel.id);`

issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds an error class and message but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state when focus lands on it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3245: `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is corrected and becomes valid, the `_renderResult` function removes the error class but fails to set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. Assistive technologies may continue to report the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3240: `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger. ARIA states should be present on load so screen readers can announce the element's capability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1615: `_initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader` module hides the header on scroll-down by adding a CSS class but does not apply `aria-hidden="true"`. Keyboard users navigating backwards or screen reader users may still encounter the "hidden" header elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: `addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters` function updates the grid and count badge but does not provide a live region announcement. Screen reader users will not know how many products remain after applying a category or text filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2215: `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` module attaches a click listener to a `zoneEl` (likely a `div`) to trigger a file upload but does not provide a `keydown` handler for the Space key. Only Enter is covered, violating WCAG 2.1.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4185: `if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `toastManager` creates a dismiss button with the text content "×" but no `aria-label`. Screen reader users will hear "Button" or "Times" instead of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1355: `const dismissBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', className: classes.dismissBtn, [ARIA.LABEL]: STRINGS.labels.dismissToast }, '×');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons in the `productFilter` module toggle the visibility of products in the grid but do not carry `aria-controls` pointing to the grid's ID.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2275: `btn.setAttribute(ARIA.CONTROLS, 'product-grid-id');`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete on personal data input

issuesFound.fix: Line 3555: `const field = createElement('input', { name: 'full_name', autocomplete: 'name' });`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, severely impacting navigation for those who rely on keyboard or assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 317: Change to :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; } or add box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus);

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule removes the focus outline for non-keyboard focus events without a replacement. Users who trigger focus via mouse or touch may lose visual feedback, degrading the experience for those with motor or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 321: Remove this rule or add a visible focus style, e.g., outline: 1px solid var(--colour-border); for :focus.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open(), close(), and toggle() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded state of the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1568: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 1574: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`. Line 1580: ensure toggle() calls open/close which now update aria-expanded.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions change the visibility of dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger element. Screen reader users cannot perceive the expanded state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1698: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 1713: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: billing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (data-period) act as a two-state toggle but do not set aria-pressed to reflect the active state. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2465: in _syncToggleState, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`. Line 2470: similarly update for _toggleTrack if applicable.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the visible product count and grid but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2094: after `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) || \`Showing ${totalMatches} products\`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function updates all plan card prices but does not announce the billing period change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the price updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2428: after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (conditionally based on period).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function in inlineValidator adds error classes and messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen reader users may not be alerted to the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3075: in the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);`. In the valid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not have aria-expanded initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is closed on page load, but the trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set initially. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1645: in the init function, after `_initialised = true;` add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers do not have aria-expanded initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are hidden on page load, but their trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded="false" set initially. Screen reader users may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1760: in the register function, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer does not have aria-hidden set when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav close function hides the drawer via CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. This leaves the drawer content in the accessibility tree when hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1574: in close(), add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);`. In open(), add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close does not announce via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open and close functions change the visibility of the navigation menu but do not announce the state change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1568: in open(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line 1574: in close(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close does not announce via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions change the visibility of dropdown menus but do not announce the state change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when dropdowns open or close.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1698: in _openInstance, add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');`. Line 1713: in _closeInstance, add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close does not announce via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions for FAQ items change the visibility of answers but do not announce the state change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when answers expand or collapse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3290: in open(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) || \`${item.question} expanded\`);`. Line 3308: in close(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) || \`${item.question} collapsed\`);`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button toggles between play and pause states but does not have aria-pressed initialized on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3418: in init(), after setting up _playBtn, add `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, _playing);`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel slide changes not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: form validation error messages not associated with aria-errormessage

issuesFound.fix: Line 3075: in the invalid branch, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.ERRORMESSAGE, errorEl.id);`. In the valid branch, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: productFilter sort change does not announce via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _onSortChange function updates the product sort order but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when sorting is applied.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button click does not announce via live region

issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent accept and decline buttons act as toggle choices but do not use aria-pressed to indicate selection. Screen reader users may not know which option is activated.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1068: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1081: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. Since the menu is closed by default, the initial state is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1134: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the `CSS.open` class and the `hidden` attribute on the drawer, but they do not update `aria-hidden`. This can leave the drawer's content discoverable by some AT even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1066: `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');`, Line 1084: `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._onViewToggle` function changes the visual layout of the product grid (grid vs list) but does not provide a live-region announcement. Users of screen readers will not be aware that the view mode has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1448: `liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${mode} view`);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing._onToggleClick` function toggles the billing period (month vs year) but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1658: `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, _pricingState.period === 'year');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds an error class and updates the error message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field when it is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2344: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes the error class but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute when the field becomes valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2338: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

fpTitles: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1156: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1167: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up event listeners for the navigation toggle but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state. This leaves the trigger without a state indicator until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1223: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader` module toggles the `CSS.headerHidden` class to hide the header on scroll-down, but it does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. The header remains in the accessibility tree while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1448: `setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`, Line 1458: `setAttr(_header, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `overflow: visible`, which does **not** hide the element from sighted users but also does **not** keep it accessible; it fails the standard screen‑reader‑only technique and may be ignored by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace the rule with the correct technique, e.g.:  
  Line 469: `position: absolute;`  
  Line 470: `width: 1px;`  
  Line 471: `height: 1px;`  
  Line 472: `padding: 0;`  
  Line 473: `margin: -1px;`  
  Line 474: `overflow: hidden;`  
  Line 475: `clip: rect(0,0,0,0);`  
  Line 476: `white-space: nowrap;`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu does not announce open/close via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing a dropdown fires custom events but no live‑region element is updated, leaving users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a hidden live‑region (e.g., `<div role="status" aria-live="polite">`) and set its `textContent` to “Menu opened” / “Menu closed” inside `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance`.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not update aria‑expanded (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The search component toggles the suggestion panel via `_searchState.open` but never sets `aria‑expanded` on the `<input>`, so screen readers cannot know whether the listbox is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_open()` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close()` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Initialise the attribute to `"false"` on page load.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search open/close not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion list opens or closes, no live‑region is updated, so users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live‑region (e.g., `
`) and set its text to “Search suggestions shown” or “Search suggestions hidden” in `_open()` and `_close()`. ---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not update aria‑expanded (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` and `close(item)` functions change the panel visibility but never modify the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute, breaking accessibility.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Initialise the attribute based on the default open state in `register()`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item does not update any live‑region, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live‑region (e.g., `
`) and set its text to “Answer expanded” / “Answer collapsed” inside `open(item)` and `close(item)`. ---

issuesFound.title: Pricing simple toggle button missing aria‑pressed updates (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off billing‑period toggle changes visual state but never updates `aria‑pressed`, leaving assistive technology unaware of the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_onToggleClick()` (or after `setPeriod`) call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'))`. Also initialise `aria‑pressed` to `"false"` in `init()`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing simple toggle missing aria‑controls (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle element controls the hidden period‑track element but never receives an `aria-controls` reference, breaking the relationship for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
After locating `_toggleTrack`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', _toggleTrack.id)` (ensure the track has an `id`). Initialise the track’s `id` if absent.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` removes error messages but never removes the `aria‑invalid="true"` attribute from a field that becomes valid, so the invalid state persists for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In `clearField(el)` (or after a successful validation) call `el.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`. Ensure `markValid` also removes the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce price update via live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: `setPeriod()` updates all plan‑card prices and related UI, but no live‑region is updated, so users are not informed that the displayed prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live‑region (e.g., `
`) and set its text to “Pricing updated to monthly/annual/quarterly” after the price updates in `setPeriod()`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle() function only adds/removes CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never sets aria‑expanded on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls and aria‑expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button never receives aria‑controls pointing to the drawer’s id and does not have an initial aria‑expanded="false", violating JS‑E.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation does not set aria‑expanded on page load (JS‑E)
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads the menu is hidden, but the trigger lacks aria‑expanded="false", so AT assumes the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add the attribute in init() as shown above (same as Issue 2).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open routine does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance adds CSS classes and removes hidden but never updates aria‑expanded on inst.trigger, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close routine does not set aria‑expanded to false on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance removes classes but leaves aria‑expanded unchanged, leaving the state ambiguous.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not receive aria‑expanded when the suggestion panel opens
issuesFound.explanation: The search box should announce that a listbox is now visible via aria‑expanded, but the attribute is never set.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not receive aria‑expanded ="false" when the panel closes
issuesFound.explanation: The input remains with no aria‑expanded state after closing, so AT cannot know the panel is hidden.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open routine does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: open() adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on item.trigger, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the answer is visible.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded when switched
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch changes the billing period but never reflects the state via aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded, violating JS‑B.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons (monthly/quarterly/yearly) do not update aria‑pressed when selected
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a period button changes the UI but never toggles aria‑pressed, so AT cannot know which period is active.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: The validator only adds error messages and CSS classes; it never adds aria‑invalid="true", so assistive tech cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid (JS‑D)
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the code removes the error message but leaves aria‑invalid set, causing a stale error state for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the else‑branch removes the attribute (as shown in Issue 11).  

---  

These twelve issue blocks satisfy all mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and address the most critical missing ARIA state updates in the script.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation does not set aria‑expanded on page load (JS‑E)
Dropdown menu close routine does not set aria‑expanded to false on its trigger
Site‑search input does not receive aria‑expanded ="false" when the panel closes

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria‑expanded on the trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The functions _openInstance and _closeInstance in the dropdownMenu controller change the panel’s visibility but never call setAttribute('aria‑expanded', …) or assign .trigger.ariaExpanded. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot perceive the open/closed state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: In register() the trigger receives aria‑haspopup and aria‑controls but never receives an initial aria‑expanded value. Assistive technologies assume the default state is unknown.
issuesFound.fix:
In register() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); so the collapsed state is explicit.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not initialise aria‑expanded on load
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.init() creates the hamburger button but never sets aria‑expanded="false" on _navToggle when the drawer is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix:
After obtaining _navToggle, add setAttr(_navToggle, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); or include the attribute in the HTML markup.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate() calls srUtils.markInvalid() which only adds aria‑describedby but does not set aria‑invalid="true". Users of assistive technology lose the error state cue.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: After a field passes validation the code only removes the error message but leaves aria‑invalid set, causing a persistent error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In srUtils.markValid() ensure field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); as shown above.

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation functions (contact form) rely on inlineValidator, thus inherit the missing aria‑invalid behaviour
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form’s fields are bound with inlineValidator.bind(), so the same aria‑invalid omission applies, leaving errors invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Apply the fix from Issue 4/5 to srUtils.markInvalid/markValid or add explicit setAttribute('aria-invalid', …) in the contact form validation path.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the numeric badge is updated but no aria‑live region is notified, so screen‑reader users are unaware of result‑count changes.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions do not announce state changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile drawer updates CSS classes but never informs AT users, leaving them unaware of the navigation state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions do not announce via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: No live‑region update occurs when a dropdown panel is shown or hidden, so screen‑reader users cannot hear the change.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel hide does not set aria‑hidden when hidden (and does not remove it when shown)
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden via setAttribute('hidden') but aria‑hidden is never toggled, leaving the element potentially still exposed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not set aria‑hidden when closed (and does not remove it when opened)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer’s visibility is controlled by CSS classes only; AT may still expose the hidden drawer.
issuesFound.fix:
In open() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false'); and in close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); .

---

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle buttons lack aria‑pressed state on initial load

issuesFound.fix: After reading the stored preference, set setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnTheme===pref)); for each button during init(). (The code already does this on change; add the same logic on load.)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button (SELECTORS.navToggle) does not have its aria-expanded attribute updated when the menu opens or closes, nor is it initialised on page load. Screen reader users will not know the current state of the menu.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden synchronization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer element (SELECTORS.navDrawer) has its visibility toggled via CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but aria-hidden is not updated to match. When closed, it remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1355: function open(announceToSR) { ... _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); ... }
  Line ~1370: function close(announceToSR) { ... _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel missing aria-hidden synchronization

issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1510: function _openInstance(inst) { ... inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); ... }
  Line ~1530: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded state management
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (SELECTORS.searchInput) acts as a combobox trigger but does not update aria-expanded when the suggestion list opens or closes, nor on initial mount.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch listbox missing aria-hidden synchronization
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion listbox element is shown/hidden via the hidden attribute, but aria-hidden is not updated. Hidden suggestions remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1850: function show() { ... _listEl.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); ... }
  Line ~1860: function hide() { ... _listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.fix: Line ~2200: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel missing aria-hidden synchronization
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ answer panels (.faq-item dd) toggle the hidden attribute but do not update aria-hidden. Collapsed answers remain accessible to screen readers.

  Line ~2220: function close(item, announceToSR) { ... item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid/markValid missing aria-invalid management

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which keeps the content in the document flow and visible, contrary to its purpose. More critically, it does not use the standard clip or clip-path technique, meaning screen reader visibility is not guaranteed and visual layout is broken. This violates the intended use of screen-reader-only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 362-374: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

This combination provides a contrast ratio of approximately 3.48:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision may have difficulty reading the button text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on product taglines
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-tagline class uses color: #5588bb on what is typically a white background, providing only 3.48:1 contrast. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for small text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1551: .product-tagline { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); margin: 0; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-wide); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on price period text
issuesFound.explanation: The .price-period class uses color: #aaaaaa (var(--colour-text-secondary)) on white background, providing only 2.32:1 contrast. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1846: .price-period { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-600); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); align-self: flex-end; margin-bottom: var(--space-1); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative for account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector inherits text-decoration: none from the global a rule and only provides a color change on hover (line 903) without underline, border-bottom, or background highlight. This relies solely on color to distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color).
issuesFound.fix: Line 895: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); text-decoration: underline; color: var(--colour-neutral-700); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative for footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links inherit text-decoration: none and only change color on hover without providing an underline, border, or other non-color visual differentiator. Users with color vision deficiencies may not perceive these as links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2181: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative for social media links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links inherit text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover to indicate interactivity. Without underline or other visual cues, users may not recognize these as clickable links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2191: .social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 28px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides only outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 312: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Within the forced-colors media query, the focus indicator is removed with outline: none and no visible replacement, leaving high-contrast mode users without any focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 323: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible state removes the default outline and sets only outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 442: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links and buttons lose their focus indicator with outline: none and only outline-offset, making it impossible for keyboard users to see their current position in the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 622: #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible, #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have no visible focus indicator due to outline: none and negative outline-offset without a replacement style, blocking keyboard navigation in submenus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 648: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 687: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button component has an explicit min-height of 32px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 439: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. This creates a significant barrier for users with motor disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 516: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-lg
issuesFound.explanation: The large button variant has a min-height of 36px, which is below the 44px recommended target size, potentially causing difficulty for users with tremors or limited precision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 523: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons have explicit min-width and min-height of 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size and creating accessibility barriers for motor-impaired users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle has explicit min-height and min-width of 28px, meeting the 24px minimum but falling short of the 44px recommended target size for comfortable activation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button

issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have an explicit min-height of 28px, meeting minimum requirements but falling below the 44px recommended size for optimal motor accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button component includes transform in its transition property and has a transform on the :active state, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 454: (add after line 454) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:active { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The button active state applies a translateY transform without a prefers-reduced-motion override, potentially causing discomfort for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 450: (add to existing media query at line 908 or create new) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition and applies translateY on hover without a reduced-motion override, potentially triggering vestibular issues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 783: (add) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hero button hover state applies a translateY transform without a reduced-motion media query to disable it for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 791: (add) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition including transform and applies perspective transforms on hover, but the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 851 is empty and does not disable these animations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 851: .hero-image img { transition: opacity var(--transition-slower); transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-2xl); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which tightens text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by making words run together visually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 147: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the visibility state of the drawer. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3520: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` inside the open function.
  Line 3540: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_open = false;` inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a FAQ item, the function sets item.open = true and shows the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never announced as true to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4510: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but it lacks the required aria-expanded attribute to indicate whether the suggestions are currently visible or collapsed on initial page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3810: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialisation at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" when the module initialises, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial closed state of the drawer on page load (WCAG 4.1.2).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3490: After `_initialised = true;` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded initialisation at page load
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown instances, the trigger button is given aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" to reflect that the panel is initially hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3590: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field fails validation, the markInvalid function adds the error message to aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2910: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function body.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid clearing on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the markValid function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute, potentially leaving stale invalid state announcements on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2930: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` as the first line inside the markValid function body.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden updates
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer is shown and hidden using CSS classes only, but the code does not toggle aria-hidden to match the visibility state. When closed, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree even though it is visually hidden, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3525: Add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` in the open function after showing the drawer.
  Line 3545: Add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` in the close function before or alongside the CSS class removal.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute referencing the drawer element id, preventing screen reader users from understanding which element is controlled by the button, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3495: After ensuring _drawer has an id (e.g., `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav');`), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId);`

issuesFound.title: pricing price updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods, the price values update visually with animation, but no aria-live announcement is made to inform screen reader users that prices have changed and what the new billing period is, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4160: After updating all plan cards (after the forEach loop), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices switched to ' + period + ' billing');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When applying filters to the product grid, the visible card count updates and the grid refreshes, but screen reader users receive no announcement about how many products match the selected filters or that the filter has been applied, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4010: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback (after _updateCountBadge), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes the visible content but does not announce the state change or the question title via the live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4515: Add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' — answer expanded');` at the end of the open function.
  Line 4555: Add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' — answer collapsed');` at the end of the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing dropdown menus reveals or hides navigation options without announcing the state change via the live region, preventing screen reader users from knowing when the menu is available, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3625: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` at the end of _openInstance.
  Line 3645: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` at the end of _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer does not trigger a live region announcement to inform screen reader users that the menu state has changed and whether it is now open or closed, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3528: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` at the end of the open function.
  Line 3558: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` at the end of the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: Although a focus trap variable is declared in the module, the mobileNav open function never initialises or activates a focus trap when the drawer opens. Keyboard focus can escape to background elements while the modal drawer is open, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3522: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` immediately after `_lockScroll();` inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the mobile drawer, the focus trap is deactivated (if it existed) but focus is not programmatically returned to the trigger button. This strands keyboard focus or sends it to the top of the document, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3555: Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` at the end of the close function, after `_unlockScroll();` and before emitting the close event.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller shows and hides panels using the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" or "false" on the trigger buttons. Assistive technologies cannot announce the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2620 (inside _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line ~2640 (inside _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) indicate selection visually via CSS classes but do not set aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to screen readers. Since these are toggle buttons, aria-pressed is required to indicate which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3830 (inside forEach): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds aria-describedby pointing to the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1507 (after addDescribedBy): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1535 (after removing describedby): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is initialized without aria-expanded="false", violating the requirement that state attributes reflect the initial collapsed state on page load (WCAG 4.1.2).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed

  Line ~2415 (in open()): Add `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` or `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel missing aria-hidden when closed

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel missing aria-hidden when closed
